title: Speaking: Making Plans
learningGoal: Engage in a conversation describing your current actions and making plans for tomorrow using the future tense.
instructions: You are talking to a friend on the phone. You will assume a FEMALE identity for this exercise. Answer the questions using continuous and future tense verbs ending in 'i'.
speakingPrompt: Speak clearly. Remember to use 'rahi' and 'ungi' since you are speaking as a female.
scenario: A phone call with a friend discussing current activities and tomorrow's plans.

subTasks.prompt: Your friend asks: 'Tum kya kar rahi ho?' (What are you doing?). Reply: 'I am eating right now.' (To eat = Khaana)
Your friend asks: 'Kal kya karogi?' (What will you do tomorrow?). Reply: 'Tomorrow I will work.' (Work = Kaam)
Your friend asks: 'Kya tum Delhi jaogi?' (Will you go to Delhi?). Reply: 'Yes, I will go to Delhi.' (Go = Jaa)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Yes, I will go to Delhi.

subTasks.prompt: Tell your friend: 'You please come tomorrow.' (Polite future request of Aa)

scenarioContext.targetText: आप फोन पर बात कर रही हैं।
scenarioContext.transliteration: Aap phone par baat kar rahi hain.
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: You are talking on the phone.

baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yes, I will go to Delhi.

acceptableParaphrases: Saying 'Tum kal aana' instead of 'Aap kal aaiyega' is acceptable but less polite.

criteria.name: Feminine Verb Usage
criteria.description: Fails if user says 'kha raha hoon', 'karunga', or 'jaunga'. Must use the 'i' endings for the female role.

criteria.name: Future Polite
criteria.description: Must attach 'ga' to the formal imperative for 'aaiyega'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Main abhi kha rahi hoon. Kal main kaam karungi. Haan, main Delhi jaungi. Aap kal aaiyega.

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! You held a conversation using complex timeline shifts and correct feminine endings.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the feminine endings. You must say 'karungi' instead of 'karunga'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Make sure you don't confuse the present continuous 'rahi' with the future 'ungi'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: मैं अभी खा रही हूँ। कल मैं काम करूँगी। हाँ, मैं दिल्ली जाऊँगी। आप कल आइयेगा।

hintText: Speak slowly to make sure you hit the 'i' vowel on 'karungi' and 'jaungi'.
commonMistakes: Defaulting to the masculine 'karunga' out of habit.
